decisive_pair_note: Layer-1 shape and both branch cards (curl_129_h1, curl_165_h1) name the decisive pair as fast>minimizer (aflfast_rarity). minimizer = PowerSchedule None (calibration-only energy); fast = AFLFast FAST n_fuzz rare-edge power schedule. Substitute naive for minimizer only if the campaign lacks a minimizer arm and naive is the confirmed flat-energy baseline.

hypotheses.label: fast over-invests havoc energy in the rare-edge seed, so its corpus shows more exploration past the rare edge than minimizer's

measurement.registry_tool_note: No built registry tool measures scheduling energy allocation. operand_enrichment is for fixed-offset VALUE gates (this is a presence/non-null gate, operand_literal null); joint_necessity is for vpc assembly-depth (no vpc arm); value_distance_reached needs operand_literal (null here); depth_reach re-reads the resolve pattern (see decidable_rationale). NEW descriptor proposed; orchestrator builds it ONLY if a per-seed energy ledger ever ships.

params.rare_edge_seed: the lowest-n_fuzz seed in fast's corpus that hits the gate's caller edge (Curl_http_host @ http.c:2157 for curl_129; Curl_http_cookies @ http.c:2792 for curl_165) but NOT the target branch
params.descendant_share: fraction of corpus seeds that are lineage-descendants of the rare-edge seed (or, if lineage parentage is unavailable, fraction whose covered-edge set is a strict superset of the rare-edge seed's) - computed identically for fast and minimizer corpora, expressed as fast / minimizer ratio
params.depth_reach: max coverage-edge depth past the rare edge reached by any descendant, fast minus minimizer

hypotheses.prediction: G1: If the win is the rare-edge POWER SCHEDULE, fast must spend disproportionate budget mutating the rare-edge seed, so fast's corpus shows MORE descendants of / deeper reach past the rare edge than minimizer's (rare_seed_descendant_share fast/minimizer >= 1.5 AND rare_seed_depth_reach > 0). A rival 'lucky seed / no scheduling effect' mechanism predicts fast and minimizer explore the rare edge equally (ratio ~ 1, depth diff ~ 0) - the win would then be variance, not the schedule. The measurement is INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern (G2): it scores corpus exploration AROUND the rare edge, not whether fast resolved the target branch.
hypotheses.decidable_rationale: G3 HONESTY: aflfast's effect is a SCHEDULING dynamic - WHICH seed gets energy WHEN, accumulated over the campaign - not a corpus-content fingerprint. fast and minimizer share a byte-identical havoc mutator vocabulary (data_realities: mutation_op cannot distinguish them) and SAVE THE SAME KINDS OF BYTES; they only budget them differently. So NO terminal-corpus tool discriminates them: the only trace of the energy boost is corpus SCALE/SHAPE, confounded by run-to-run variance, total exec budget, and seed-admission luck. With n=2 branches on ONE target (curl) and no per-seed energy ledger in seed_lineage, 'more descendants past the rare edge' cannot be cleanly attributed to the power schedule vs ordinary stochastic drift. A per-seed ENERGY LEDGER (exec count attributed per seed over time) would make this decidable, but the campaign ships none, and a seed_lineage scheduling-event descriptor is FORBIDDEN by data_realities (same class as the 'I2S event'). Mark decidable:false; fallback carries these branches to inconclusive.

data_realities_respected: Not a VALUE gate (presence_or_nonnull, operand_literal null) -> operand_enrichment (byte_freq_ratio) returns no_gate_signature; inapplicable, not proposed. It also scores cmplog-vs-naive enrichment, while the decisive pair here is fast-vs-minimizer (both lack I2S) - doubly inapplicable.
Not a vpc joint-assembly gate, no vpc arm -> joint_necessity (struct_size_and_token_count) inapplicable; not proposed.
operand_literal is null -> value_distance_reached has no target operand to compute Hamming distance against; inapplicable, not proposed.

depth_reach is CIRCULAR here (G2): it reads how deep the WINNER-resolving seed set drives the branch vs the LOSER-blocking set - i.e. exactly the W/L resolve split that DEFINES this shape - and the winner's seeds ARE the seeds fast evolved via the very energy boost under test. A deeper reading just re-confirms fast resolved it; it cannot separate the schedule from a lucky seed, and it does not even contrast fast vs minimizer. Not proposed.

corpus_size_ratio / token_count would measure only corpus SCALE, which is variance / exec-budget confounded and not attributable to the schedule per-branch; inventing such a ratio to force a label would violate G3. Not proposed.
mutation_op is byte-identical / untagged across fuzzers -> NO seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence 'scheduling event' descriptor (infeasible, same class as the forbidden 'I2S event').

Both branches are curl (on-disk corpus on s4), so a corpus/lineage descriptor is at least populatable, but n=2 single-target is too thin to power any rule and the signal itself is variance-confounded.
notes: ROUND-3 (FINAL) RE-DESIGN VERDICT: WHOLE-SHAPE decidable:false (UNCHANGED from rounds 1 and 2) -> both UNLABELED branches (curl_129, curl_165) route to inconclusive honestly (G3). FINAL RE-CONFIRMATION per brief: I independently re-checked EVERY built tool not yet tried (operand_enrichment/byte_freq_ratio, joint_necessity/struct_size_and_token_count, value_distance_reached, depth_reach, corpus_size_ratio, token_count) for a concrete discriminating signal on these two presence/non-null (operand_literal null) gates whose decisive pair is fast-vs-minimizer. FINDING (confirms rounds 1-2): NONE applies, and no built-tool signal was missed in either prior round. operand_enrichment + value_distance_reached both require an operand literal these presence gates lack AND target the I2S/VP value axis, not scheduling. joint_necessity requires a vpc assembly-depth gate (none). depth_reach is G2-circular: it reads the winner-vs-loser seed depth, which IS the resolve split that defined the shape, and its winner seeds are the product of the very energy boost under test - it cannot tell the power schedule from a lucky seed, nor does it contrast fast vs minimizer at all. corpus_size_ratio / token_count would only measure corpus scale (variance / exec-budget confounded), not attributable to the schedule per-branch. PRIOR KEPT verbatim: rare_edge_energy_overdrive (the only prior hypothesis) remains decidable:false; no relaxed-threshold re-test (forbidden). NEW MECHANISMS: none proposed - there is no genuinely different, falsifiable AND discriminating mechanism a BUILT tool can score for these scheduling branches. ROOT OBSTRUCTION: aflfast-rarity is a non-local SCHEDULING technique whose advantage (FAST n_fuzz rare-edge power schedule, up to ~4x energy on rarely-hit buckets vs minimizer's PowerSchedule None calibration-only energy) leaves NO per-branch content fingerprint - fast and minimizer save the same byte kinds and differ only in WHICH seed gets budget WHEN (data_realities). WHAT WOULD MAKE IT DECIDABLE: a per-seed energy/exec ledger attributed over campaign time (seed_lineage:energy_share) - not present, and a lineage scheduling-event descriptor is explicitly forbidden by data_realities. COLLAPSE: the two members describe ONE identical mechanism (rare-edge power-schedule havoc amplification on a Host-header/cookie path); nothing to discriminate between them. This is a legitimately non-discriminable scheduling family; honest inconclusive is the correct outcome (G3) and is the FINAL verdict. Round-0 also raised a fast/minimizer synthetic-harness execution caveat (project memory: fast/minimizer execute 0 on synthetic harnesses) - independently blocking a synthetic re-derivation - but the controlling reason is the terminal-corpus invisibility above, valid for ALL servers.
